entries.definition:
فَرِيًّا they said, ‘Mary, you have done something terrible!’ [also
rendered by commentators as: wonderful, unheard of, strange, of
great magnitude, of great gravity, something false].
entries.definition_english:
فَرِيًّا they said, ‘Mary, you have done something terrible!’ [also
rendered by commentators as: wonderful, unheard of, strange, of
great magnitude, of great gravity, something false].

entries.definition:
f–z–z to unsettle, to dislodge, to frighten, to disturb; to
overcome; to ooze. Of this root, two forms occur three times in
the Qur’an: يَسْتَفِزّ yastafizz twice and اسْتَفْزِز ʾistafziz once.

يَسْتَفِزّ yastafizz [imperf. of v. X, trans.; imper. اسْتَفْزِز ʾistafziz] to
unsettle, to dislodge, to scare away, to unsettle (17:103) فَأَرَادَ أَنْ
يَسْتَفِزَّهُمْ مِنَ الْأَرْضِ فَأَغْرَقْنَاهُ وَمَنْ مَعَهُ جَمِيعًا so he wanted to dislodge them
from the land, but We drowned him and those with him together.
entries.definition_english:
f–z–z to unsettle, to dislodge, to frighten, to disturb; to
overcome; to ooze. Of this root, two forms occur three times in
the Qur’an: يَسْتَفِزّ yastafizz twice and اسْتَفْزِز ʾistafziz once.

يَسْتَفِزّ yastafizz [imperf. of v. X, trans.; imper. اسْتَفْزِز ʾistafziz] to
unsettle, to dislodge, to scare away, to unsettle (17:103) فَأَرَادَ أَنْ
يَسْتَفِزَّهُمْ مِنَ الْأَرْضِ فَأَغْرَقْنَاهُ وَمَنْ مَعَهُ جَمِيعًا so he wanted to dislodge them
